body.purpose: Default reading line. List rows in default state, modal summary, panel header label.

heading.purpose: Panel titles, modal heading, active filter chip, confirm-button label. TextAttributes.BOLD on top of body fg.

margin-note.purpose: All secondary copy. Counts, hints, deferred rows, footer binding descriptions, trailing hint copy. Default attribute on margin fg.

inverse-chip.purpose: Footer label chip only. BOLD attribute on page fg over the-mark bg.

motion.name: toast-decay
motion.purpose: Toast and event-rail messages fade by removal after four seconds. No interpolated animation; the row simply disappears.

motion.name: transition-instant
motion.purpose: All other state changes (accept, defer, reject, focus shift, filter cycle) re-render immediately. The terminal cannot ease; the design does not pretend it can.

breakpoints.name: min-cells
breakpoints.purpose: Floor for legibility. Below this, panels overflow gracefully via flexbox; layouts do not reflow.

breakpoints.name: comfortable
breakpoints.purpose: Target for the Discovery and Log panels. Three-column help drawer assumes at least this width.

components.name: Panel Header
components.description: One row at the top of every panel. Title in BOLD on the left, dim summary on the right.
components.css: .ds-panel-header { display: flex; flex-direction: row; justify-content: space-between; padding: 0 1ch; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; } .ds-panel-header__title { font-weight: 700; color: var(--ds-body, #e5e5e5); } .ds-panel-header__summary { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); }

components.name: List Row (default + focused + status)
components.description: Three rows showing the full row vocabulary: default, focused (with cursor glyph), and an accepted/success row.
components.html:
1. ▶ .config/fish 3 pending
  2. › ▼ .config/fish 3 pending A·D·X
  3. near config.fish accepted
components.css: .ds-list { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; } .ds-row { padding: 0 1ch; white-space: pre; color: var(--ds-body, #e5e5e5); } .ds-row--focused { color: var(--ds-the-mark, #ffff00); } .ds-row--success { color: var(--ds-the-approval, #00ff00); } .ds-row--danger { color: var(--ds-the-correction, #ff0000); } .ds-row--deferred { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); }

components.name: Filter Chip (active + inactive)
components.description: Filter strip pattern used in the discovery panel. Active is bracketed and BOLD; inactive is unbracketed and dim.
components.html: [pending 7] accepted 12 deferred 3 rejected 0 all 22
components.css: .ds-chips { display: inline-flex; gap: 1ch; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; padding: 0 1ch; } .ds-chip { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); white-space: pre; } .ds-chip--active { color: var(--ds-the-approval, #00ff00); font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Footer Label Chip + Bindings
components.description: Single footer row. Reverse-fg label chip on the left (the only inverse region in the chrome), then the active panel's bindings, then a global help hint, separated by mid-dot.
components.html: discoverj/k move · a/A accept · ? more
components.css: .ds-footer { display: flex; flex-direction: row; align-items: center; height: 1lh; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; } .ds-footer__chip { background: var(--ds-the-mark, #ffff00); color: var(--ds-page, #0a0a0a); font-weight: 700; padding: 0 1ch; } .ds-footer__bindings { padding-left: 1ch; flex: 1; min-width: 0; overflow: hidden; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-footer__key { color: var(--ds-the-mark, #ffff00); } .ds-footer__desc { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); } .ds-footer__sep { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); }

components.name: Confirm Modal
components.description: Centered confirmation modal. Double-border surface, BOLD title, body summary, dim path bullets, action row with bracketed labels, dim hint line. Used for every destructive op.
components.html:
Reject subtree
Reject 3 files under ~/.config/fish?
• config.fish
• functions/fzf.fish
• completions/jj.fish
backup → ~/.dotfiles.bak/9c41/2026-05-04T01-23/
[Reject][Cancel]
enter/y confirm · esc/n cancel
components.css: .ds-modal { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1lh; padding: 2ch; background: var(--ds-elevated-verso-dark, #2a2a2a); color: var(--ds-body, #e5e5e5); border: 2px double var(--ds-body, #e5e5e5); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; } .ds-modal__title { font-weight: 700; } .ds-modal__summary { } .ds-modal__path { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); } .ds-modal__backup { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); } .ds-modal__actions { display: flex; flex-direction: row; gap: 2ch; margin-top: 1lh; } .ds-modal__confirm { color: var(--ds-the-approval, #00ff00); font-weight: 700; } .ds-modal__cancel { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); } .ds-modal__hint { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); }

components.name: Toast Rail
components.description: Single-row event rail anchored to the bottom of the panel body. Tone is success / default / danger; pairs with an optional [u] undo affordance.
components.html: accepted ~/.config/fish (3 files) · 4 pending[u] undo
components.css: .ds-toast { display: flex; flex-direction: row; justify-content: space-between; padding: 0 1ch; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; } .ds-toast__msg--success { color: var(--ds-the-approval, #00ff00); } .ds-toast__msg--danger { color: var(--ds-the-correction, #ff0000); } .ds-toast__msg--default { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); } .ds-toast__undo { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); }

components.name: Help Drawer Row
components.description: Three-column key/desc rows that fill the help drawer. Key in the-mark, desc in margin, padded so keys align within a column.
components.html:
Discover
j/k move
a/A accept
d/D defer
X reject group
/ search
u undo
components.css: .ds-help { padding: 1lh 2ch; background: var(--ds-verso-dark, #0a0a0a); color: var(--ds-body, #e5e5e5); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; } .ds-help__section { color: var(--ds-the-mark, #ffff00); font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 1lh; } .ds-help__cols { display: flex; flex-direction: row; gap: 4ch; } .ds-help__col { display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .ds-help__row { white-space: pre; } .ds-help__key { color: var(--ds-the-mark, #ffff00); } .ds-help__desc { color: var(--ds-margin, #7f7f7f); }

narrative.overview: ldf reads like a typeset record of a working life. The jj op log is its manuscript: every change carries a description, a parent, and a hand. Surfaces are pages, focus is a margin mark, errors are red-pencil corrections, and secondary copy lives in the gutter. The reader is trusted; the chrome is absent. This system explicitly rejects the four lanes named in PRODUCT.md: the generic lazy-family TUI clone, the Tokyo Night / Catppuccin pastel sea, the hacker-maximalist ASCII flourish, and the SaaS dashboard hero-metric chrome. Voice carries through visually. Composed, exact, quiet. Hierarchy comes from weight and rhythm, not borders or color. Color appears only when it changes the meaning of the row.
keyCharacteristics: Type does the layout work. Bold for emphasis, dim for secondary, default for body, color for state.
Borders are an admission, not a default. Two exceptions: the modal surface (border.emphasis, double) and any boxed input.
One chrome chip exists on the whole shell — the active panel label in the footer, set in BOLD on a reversed-fg ground.
Status is signaled at the row level, never through a separate badge column.
Empty states are two lines: a default-weight statement and a dim hint.

rules.name: The ANSI-Only Rule
rules.body: No hex literals in views. The codebase enforces this with a unit test (views/theme/no-hex-literals.test.ts); any color leaving the theme module MUST come from RGBA.fromIndex(0–15) or RGBA.defaultForeground/Background(). A hex string anywhere under views/ is a merge blocker.

rules.name: The Color-Means-State Rule
rules.body: A row's color is its state — focused, ok, warning, deferred, error. If a row's color does not change the user's understanding of what it is, drop the color and use type weight instead.

rules.name: The One Chip Rule
rules.body: The reversed-fg label chip in the footer is the only inverse-color region in the chrome. A second chip, anywhere, is wrong.

rules.name: The Type-Carries-Rank Rule
rules.body: Sequence of fallbacks for emphasis: (1) weight (BOLD); (2) color role; (3) spacing or alignment; (4) a border, last resort. If you find yourself adding a border to make something read as important, you skipped a step.

rules.name: The No-Italics Rule
rules.body: Italics in a terminal monospace are unreliable across themes. Some render obliquely, some not at all. Don't reach for them. Use weight + color, never italics, for emphasis.

rules.name: The Two-Line Empty-State Rule
rules.body: Every empty state is a default-weight sentence followed by a dim hint. Three lines is too many; one line is too few.

rules.name: The Two-Region Depth Rule
rules.body: If a region is not the footer label chip or a modal, it is on bg.default with no border. No card-as-container. No nested surface tints. Depth is a switch with two positions.

rules.name: The Double-Border-Means-Modal Rule
rules.body: border.emphasis is reserved for modals and the discovery-error inset. A double border anywhere else is a mis-signal — the user expects modal semantics where there are none.

dos: Do read color from useTheme() for every fg / bg / border. The theme module is the only place ANSI tokens live.
Do signal focus with the › cursor glyph plus the-mark foreground on the entire row. Cursor + color together; never one alone.
Do repeat status as a word when it carries meaning (accepted, rejected). A monochrome reader must read the same line.
Do keep the chrome to one row of footer plus one chip. The active panel label is the only inverse-fg region in the whole shell.
Do fold 'card' content into a header row (tracked · queued · sync) separated by ·. PRD 'cards' are typographic groups, not boxes.
Do use space.sm (1ch), space.md (2ch), space.lg (4ch) as the rhythm. Don't invent intermediate values.
Do make modals carry border.emphasis and bg.elevated. That pair is the modal signal; preserve it.
Do finish empty states in two lines: default-weight statement, dim hint.
donts: Don't ship a generic lazy-family TUI clone. No bordered tri-pane, no cyan/magenta status bar, no single-letter q/?/r row floating alone.
Don't introduce Tokyo Night, Catppuccin, or any pastel-on-dark palette. The placeholder hex stubs from ADR-002's example were never the destination; the ANSI token system replaced them.
Don't add hacker-maximalist ASCII: no banner art, no scanlines, no neon, no figlet.
Don't build SaaS-dashboard chrome: no hero-metric template, no card grid of icon + heading + text, no gradient anything.
Don't use border-left / border-right greater than 1 column as a colored stripe. OpenTUI does not make this easy; keep it that way.
Don't wrap regions in a card to 'group' them. Use spacing (gap, padding) and a margin-fg label instead.
Don't put italics in body copy or labels. Some terminal themes render them obliquely, some not at all. Emphasis is BOLD only.
Don't introduce a second inverse-fg region. The footer label chip is the one chip.
Don't open a modal when an inline empty state, toast, or filter strip would do. Modals are reserved for confirmation of destructive operations.
Don't hard-code width/height for layout flow. Constitution §2.2 bans it; flexbox is the only path.
Don't rely on color alone for state. Pair every color with a glyph (›, […], •) or a literal status word.
Don't decorate with color. If a color does not change the meaning of the line, it should not be there.
